user.title: Connect to an rtl_433 server
user.description: Enter the WebSocket connection details for your rtl_433 HTTP server (started with `-F http`). Use plain `ws://` by default, or enable Secure to use `wss://` through a TLS reverse proxy. No authentication is performed by this integration.

data_description.host: Hostname or IP address of the rtl_433 server.
data_description.port: TCP port of the rtl_433 HTTP server (default 8433).
data_description.path: WebSocket endpoint path on the server (default /ws).
data_description.secure: Connect over wss:// instead of ws:// (for a TLS reverse proxy).

confirm.title: Add discovered rtl_433 device
confirm.description:
A new device was observed on your rtl_433 server:

**{model}** (`{device_key}`)

Do you want to add it? Dismiss this card to ignore the device and stop it from being suggested again.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the host, port, and path, and that the rtl_433 server is reachable.

init.title: rtl_433 options
init.description: Configure discovery and the availability timeout. For a hub, set the new-device discovery toggle and the default availability timeout. For a device, override the availability timeout (leave blank to use the hub default).

data_description.discovery_enabled: When on, newly observed devices on this server are surfaced for confirmation.
data_description.availability_timeout: Mark a device unavailable after this many seconds without an event.

server_unreachable.title: rtl_433 server unreachable

abort.not_required: The rtl_433 server is reachable again; no action is required.

server_unreachable.description: Home Assistant cannot reach the rtl_433 server for **{title}**. Check that the server is running with `-F http` and that the host, port, and path are correct and reachable. The integration keeps retrying, and this issue clears automatically once the connection is restored.
